title: Pneumonanthe pannonica
taxonomy: Pneumonanthe pannonica is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Gentianaceae. It is a perennial plant native to Central and Eastern Europe. The genus Pneumonanthe is a part of the tribe Gentianeae, which includes several species of gentian plants. Pneumonanthe pannonica is closely related to other species within the same genus, such as Pneumonanthe anglica. The plant's classification is based on its morphological characteristics and genetic analysis.

intro: Pneumonanthe pannonica, commonly known as Lungwort, is a perennial plant belonging to the Boraginaceae family. It has been traditionally used to treat various respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, due to its anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties, which help to alleviate congestion and promote mucus discharge.
